source: real `tailscale status --json` stdout on the dev laptop (Tailscale 1.94.2, BackendState Running, 3 peers) — the exact byte stream the U4.5 key-expiry tier's bounded exec consumes
redactions: node public keys (nodekey:<64 hex>) -> same-length hex fill, uniquified per key (JSON Peer-map keys must stay distinct)
tailnet MagicDNS suffix + tailnet name -> same-length placeholders (grammar-valid *.ts.net kept)
hostnames / DNSNames / DisplayName -> same-length x/0 fill (punctuation + case class preserved)
tailscale IPv4 (100.x CGNAT), IPv6 (fd7a:), public endpoint ip:port, LAN ips -> same-length digit fill (IP grammar preserved)
profile/user URLs -> same-length fill after the scheme
note: KeyExpiry values (Self + all peers) are UNREDACTED — they are the parse target. All structural bytes (whitespace, key order, field presence) byte-identical to the live capture; redactions are same-length so offsets are preserved. IP/email/tailnet-SHAPED placeholder values are deliberately retained so the content-scrub test proves such rows never reach parser output.
